title: Kerala polls 2026: 20 seats that could shape the state’s verdict
content: As Kerala heads to the polls, the election narrative shifts from rallies to booth-level battles. The ruling LDF aims for a historic third term, while the UDF seeks to leverage local body momentum against anti-incumbency. The NDA is focused on converting key pockets into durable political footholds, making these 20 constituencies the state's pressure points.

title: 'Flying Bam Bahadur’s pigeon': PSL trolled over 'peacemaker act' amid US-Iran ceasefire
content: A symbolic dove release for peace at the PSL 2026 match between Peshawar Zalmi and Hyderabad Kingsmen drew significant online criticism. Critics labelled the 'Pakistan - The Peace Maker' initiative as forced and PR-driven, deeming it out of place during the cricket tournament amidst geopolitical tensions.

title: Meta releases first-ever AI model from one of its highest-paid teams, after spending billions
content: Meta has launched Muse Spark, its first AI model from Meta Superintelligence Labs, aiming to catch up with competitors. This natively multimodal reasoning model powers the Meta AI assistant and is designed for complex reasoning and everyday tasks.  While initially smaller and faster, Meta plans further generations to enhance its capabilities.

title: Anthropic CEO says AI will take your job;but his own company find enough people to work
content: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei is warning that AI could eliminate more than 50% of entry-level white-collar jobs within five years. But inside his own company, the picture looks different. Claude Code creator Boris Cherny says the software engineer job title is already dying — and Anthropic's Head of Growth says they can't hire enough PMs to keep up. The disruption isn't coming. It's already here.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office collection Day 21: Ranveer Singh starrer dips under Rs 10 crore
content: Aditya Dhar's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its record-breaking spree, crossing Rs 1,000 crore net in India and achieving a worldwide gross of Rs 1,653.67 crore. Despite a slight dip on its 21st day, the film has secured a spot in the top 10 highest-grossing films globally for 2026, marking a significant international triumph for Indian cinema.

title: IPL: Axar Patel’s gesture towards distraught David Miller wins hearts — watch
content: Delhi Capitals faced a crushing one-run loss to Gujarat Titans after David Miller's valiant effort.  Despite a dramatic chase, a crucial decision on the penultimate ball led to defeat.  However, Delhi Capitals skipper Axar Patel's empathetic gesture towards a dejected David Miller stole the spotlight, showcasing sportsmanship amidst intense competition.

title: Did ChatGPT come first or Gemini: Google CEO Sundar Pichai answers
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai stated the company did not miss the AI chatbot wave but deliberately delayed its release. He revealed Google had a functional version of ChatGPT internally, but it was deemed too toxic and unrefined for public release, prioritizing product quality over being first to market.

title: Hormuz open or close? Only a 'trickle' of oil leaving right now despite ceasefire
content: The Strait of Hormuz remains a flashpoint amid escalating Middle East tensions, with oil shipments struggling to resume normal flow. Experts suggest any impact on US fuel prices will be delayed, as a fragile ceasefire shows little progress. Global energy supplies face continued pressure, keeping oil markets unsettled despite recent price dips.

title: Artemis II crew captures stunning Earthrise and Milky Way views from lunar orbit
content: Nasa's Artemis II astronauts have returned a stunning Earthrise image. The crew also photographed the Milky Way. Their lunar flyby marked a significant milestone, reaching a record distance from Earth. The mission included observing a solar eclipse and lunar features. Artemis II is now returning for a splashdown, advancing Nasa's lunar exploration goals.

title: US-Iran ceasefire: Was Israel kept out of talks till the end?
content: Israel expressed dissatisfaction with the US over its handling of the Iran ceasefire, feeling informed late and excluded from consultations. Despite public support from Prime Minister Netanyahu, who set clear limits, concerns remain about the agreement's scope, particularly regarding Lebanon. Tensions persist, with Hezbollah launching rockets following Israeli strikes.

title: Watch: Daniil Medvedev smashes racquet seven times in ‘double bagel’ loss
content: Former world No. 1 Daniil Medvedev suffered a shocking 6-0, 6-0 defeat to Matteo Berrettini at the Monte Carlo Masters. Medvedev's frustration boiled over, leading to a racquet-smashing outburst and a code violation. Berrettini, ranked 90th, delivered a career-best performance in the swift, one-sided match, marking a rare 'double bagel' against a top-10 player.

title: Stock market today: Nifty50 opens below 23,900; BSE Sensex down over 500 points
content: Stock market today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex opened in red on Thursday, a day after the stock market saw a record single day gain. While Nifty50 went below 23,900, BSE Sensex was down over 500 points.

title: Serena Williams shares rare Easter pics, opens up on motherhood journey
content: Serena Williams shared rare Easter photos with Alexis Ohanian and daughters Olympia and Adira, calling family time everything. She also spoke on Today about motherhood, her daughters’ personalities, and learning to balance attention between them. Her words and photos showed a simple, honest side of life after tennis, focused fully on family and everyday moments.

title: Megan Thee Stallion recalls emotional moment amid Klay Thompson romance
content: The Dallas Mavericks’ star player, Klay Thompson, has proven his love and loyalty towards the popular Grammy winner, Megan Thee Stallion, for close to a year of their high profile romance. While Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson seem happy together, the popular artist had gone through a lot in her life, including witnessing her mother’s brutal death in 2019.

title: Mike Vrabel & Dianna Russini Drama Intensifies Over Scot McCloughan Affair
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and Dianna Russini of The New York Times have found themselves in major trouble after photos of them at an upscale hotel sparked outrage and taken the world by storm. The two were spotted relaxing together and sharing a hug which has convinced fans that they are having an alleged affair. Though both Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini have denied such rumors, an alleged old affair of hers has resurfaced.

title: Puducherry elections: Robot ‘Nila’ greets voters as tight NDA–Congress battle unfolds
content: Puducherry's assembly elections commenced today with an innovative touch as a robot named 'Nila' greeted voters. Approximately 9.5 lakh electors are choosing among 294 candidates across 30 constituencies. The main contenders are the NDA, led by Chief Minister N Rangasamy, and the Congress-DMK alliance. Key issues like statehood and unemployment are prominent, with counting set for May 4.

title: Sunjay Kapur's mother breaks silence after Priya Kapur removes her from RK family trust
content: Following Sunjay Kapur's death, his mother Rani Kapur is locked in a Rs 30,000 crore inheritance dispute with his widow, Priya Sachdev. Rani alleges Priya is trying to seize her late husband's assets and has been removed from the RK family trust. She expresses deep personal distress and fear, stating no settlement talks have occurred.

title: 'Interesting thought': Raghav Chadha hints at own political party
content: Sidelined AAP leader Raghav Chadha has fueled speculation about his political future after responding to a social media video suggesting he launch his own party. This comes after the AAP removed him as deputy leader in the Rajya Sabha, with Chadha stating he was "silenced, not defeated."

title: DC vs GT: What was David Miller thinking — why didn’t he run?
content: David Miller's decision to refuse a single in the final over of the IPL 2026 match against Gujarat Titans cost Delhi Capitals a potential win. Despite an injured hand, Miller's late surge brought them close, but his final act left fans questioning his judgment as they lost by one run.

title: Top stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Top stock market recommendations: Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC, Bank of India, and Cummins India have been picked as top stocks to buy today by Aakash K Hindocha, Deputy Vice President - WM Research of Nuvama Professional Clients Group.

title: Watch: 5 runs off one ball! Nehra fumes over costly overthrow by Gill
content: Gujarat Titans coach Ashish Nehra's animated reaction to a costly overthrow during Delhi Capitals' chase became a talking point. A deflected ball gifted five runs, nearly costing GT a tense one-run victory. This incident underscored how crucial even minor fielding lapses can be in tightly contested matches, highlighting the fine margins that decide outcomes.

title: Crude prices climb back towards $97 as Strait of Hormuz remains under pressure
content: Oil prices edged higher Thursday after a significant daily drop, as Middle East tensions and uncertainty surrounding the Strait of Hormuz kept markets on edge. Despite conflicting reports on tanker movement, US Vice President Vance indicated the strait was beginning to reopen. Prices remain elevated due to persistent concerns over potential renewed hostilities.

title: Candidates: How R Vaishali crushed Divya Deshmukh in 31 moves to join lead
content: Indian chess sensation R Vaishali secured a commanding victory over compatriot Divya Deshmukh at the Women's Candidates, seizing a co-lead. Meanwhile, R Praggnanandhaa faced another draw, struggling to convert his advantage against Wei Yi. In the Open section, Javokhir Sindarov leads the pack with 7/9 points. The tournament continues with crucial pairings in both sections.

title: Netanyahu warns 'finger on trigger', calls ceasefire ‘not the end’
content: Israel's Prime Minister Netanyahu declared a fragile two-week ceasefire with Iran, emphasizing readiness to resume military action if objectives aren't met. Despite cooperation with the US-Iran truce, Israel will continue striking Hezbollah. Meanwhile, 254 deaths were reported in Lebanon from Israeli strikes.

title: VP Vance will lead US team for truce talks with Iran in Pakistan: White House
content: Vice President JD Vance will lead a US delegation to Pakistan this weekend for mediated talks with Iran, aiming to secure a permanent ceasefire. The discussions in Islamabad follow a two-week truce, with the White House emphasizing the need for the Strait of Hormuz to remain open. Lebanon is not included in the current ceasefire agreement.

title: After SIR, number of voters in Mamata Banerjee's seat down 25%
content: Bhowanipore constituency, the high-profile seat of Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee, has seen its electorate shrink by nearly 25% after electoral roll revisions. Over 51,000 deletions occurred, with a significant portion of Muslims marked for adjudication and eventual deletion, raising concerns among researchers.

title: IPL 2026: Jos Buttler scripts history, enters elite six-hitting club
content: Jos Buttler achieved a significant milestone, hitting his 600th six in T20 cricket. He joined an elite group of players with this achievement. Gujarat Titans secured a thrilling one-run victory over Delhi Capitals. KL Rahul's strong performance for Delhi was not enough to secure the win. The match saw impressive batting displays from both sides.

title: Tamil Nadu chief secretary, DGP shifted; Stalin cries foul
content: The Election Commission replaced Tamil Nadu's Chief Secretary and DGP ahead of polls, citing complaints of administrative bias towards the ruling DMK. Chief Minister M.K. Stalin vehemently criticized the EC, accusing it of acting as a BJP 'branch' and attempting to hinder DMK's victory. He questioned the sudden change in assessment of the state's administration.

title: Delhi records coolest April day in 10 years
content: Delhi experienced its coolest April day in a decade, with temperatures plummeting to 28.2°C due to light rain and overcast skies. While a welcome respite, temperatures are set to climb significantly, potentially reaching 38°C by April 14. The recent rainfall also improved air quality, though a dip back to moderate is expected.

title: Sabarimala PIL was entertained in 2006, but would have been dismissed now: SC
content: The Supreme Court expressed concern over the misuse of Public Interest Litigation for personal agendas, suggesting it would have dismissed a PIL challenging the Sabarimala custom. Justices noted that such challenges, if not brought by devotees, lack cause of action. The court emphasized the need for strict locus standi rules to prevent PIL abuse, especially in religious matters.

title: ‘You can’t talk like this,’ observer shoots back at CEC Gyanesh Kumar, gets removed
content: A heated exchange erupted during a high-level election review meeting when a senior observer challenged the Chief Election Commissioner. The observer was subsequently removed, with the EC citing professional incompetence for failing to provide basic details about polling booths. The meeting also addressed vulnerable booths and assured facilities at polling stations.

title: Woman murdered, body found inside bed box in Delhi; 3 including lover arrested
content: A married woman with three children was found murdered and hidden in a storage box at a Delhi PG accommodation. Police arrested her alleged lover, Deepak, and two relatives, suspecting a dispute escalated to violence. Evidence suggests accomplices helped conceal the crime. The family had warned her about the relationship.

title: Appointment of priests by govt boards can't be a secular act: Centre
content: Solicitor General Tushar Mehta criticized the Supreme Court's past rulings on temple priest appointments, arguing they wrongly classified a religious decision as secular. He urged a review of jurisprudence, emphasizing state non-interference in religious matters and advocating for legislative control over social and religious reforms.

title: Sanitation workers appointed as BLOs for pre-SIR exercise in Hyderabad
content: Hyderabad's old city residents seeking voter list assistance encountered sanitation workers instead of trained officials. These workers, appointed due to staff shortages, lacked the necessary skills for the pre-SIR mapping exercise. They were equipped with smartphones to record voter details. Officials stated this is a temporary measure and replacements are expected soon.

title: West Bengal polls: No Muslim name finds place in BJP's list
content: The BJP has not nominated any Muslim candidates in the Bengal elections, a departure from previous polls despite a significant Muslim membership. Party leaders cited 'winnability' as the primary criterion for candidate selection, suggesting a disconnect between the BJP's platform and Muslim voting patterns. In contrast, Congress, TMC, and the Left Front have fielded a considerable number of Muslim candidates.

title: Pawan Khera seeks bail from Telangana high court in Himanta Sarma case
content: Congress leader Pawan Khera has sought anticipatory bail from the Telangana High Court. This follows a case filed by Guwahati police concerning his allegations against Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma's wife. Khera claims the FIR copy hasn't been provided and the charges are invalid. Assam Police's pursuit has now extended to Hyderabad.

title: Scottie Scheffler brings different vibes to Augusta than other star golfers
content: Scheffler might not generate the same buzz as players like Tiger and Phil, but he's a different kind of superstar, and maybe that's a good thing.

title: 2026 Masters: Experts' picks and betting tips
content: Who do our golf experts think will win? Where does our betting expert believe there's value? We break it all down.

title: OKC locks up No. 1 seed, home court throughout
content: The Thunder clinched the NBA's best regular-season record with a 128-110 win over the Clippers on Wednesday night, securing homecourt advantage throughout the postseason in their title defense.

title: Bryson: Using 3D printer-made iron at Masters
content: Bryson DeChambeau confirmed to ESPN that he'll play the Masters with a 5-iron that he fabricated himself with a 3D printer.

title: Cousins: I shouldn't play if not Raiders' best QB
content: Raiders quarterback Kirk Cousins is aware that Las Vegas is expected to draft Fernando Mendoza and says he only wants to play if it's determined he's the best option.

title: Full of thanks, UCLA celebrates title at Pauley
content: Lauren Betts and her UCLA teammates celebrated the Bruins' first NCAA women's basketball championship with their fans at Pauley Pavilion on Wednesday night.

title: Reports: NCAA to discuss 5-year eligibility model
content: An NCAA panel is scheduled to discuss changes to eligibility rules that would incorporate age into the process, sources said Wednesday.

title: Prosecutors seek Tiger's prescription drug records
content: Prosecutors want the times Tiger Woods' prescriptions were filled, the number of pills, the dosage amounts and any instructions that accompanied the pills, such as warnings about driving while taking them.

title: Prized recruit Hiller, No. 2 OT in '27, picks Florida
content: Maxwell Hiller, the No. 2 offensive tackle and No. 7 overall recruit in the SC Next Junior 300, gave Florida and new head coach Jon Sumrall his verbal pledge Wednesday.

title: Rai 'wins,' young Fleetwood steals show at Par 3
content: Eight-year-old Frankie Fleetwood stole the show at Wednesday's family-friendly Masters Par 3 Contest, which saw Aaron Rai win and four holes-in-one.

title: 📈 Ranking top men's hoops players in the portal
content: More than 1,000 players have entered the portal less than 24 hours after it opened. Here's the top talent.

title: Our hottest hot takes two weeks into the MLB season
content: From a shocking NL East champion to a 50-homer slugger you probably haven't heard of yet, our experts go bold with their early predictions.

title: The art of taking a lead: Why every step matters in baseball's game of inches
content: A bad lead helped keep the Blue Jays from a title. Tim Kurkjian explores the state of a critical skill.

title: Inside the 'strange phenomenon' the NBA is struggling to contain
content: With tanking strategies growing in both audaciousness and frequency of use, can the league get its arms around the often-effective practice?

title: Stick or sack? Slot's Liverpool future dominates UCL talking points
content: Was Wednesday's 2-0 loss Liverpool's worst performance under Arne Slot? ESPN's Mark Ogden, Julien Laurens, Gab Marcotti and Sam Tighe weigh in on that and all the other moments that mattered from the UCL quarterfinal first legs.

title: Champions League recap: Atleti go past 10-man Barça, PSG defeat Liverpool
content: Both Atlético Madrid and Paris Saint-Germain get 2-0 first-leg wins on a day of quarterfinals action in the UEFA Champions League.

title: Free agency: Predictions, biggest questions, more
content: Who are the biggest names who might be on the move, and how will the condensed offseason impact free agency?

title: Projecting NFL draft quarterback landing spots, from No. 1 to No. 215: New homes for nine passers
content: Ben Solak projects landing spots for the nine QBs he considers draftable in the 2026 class (and one trade) and sizes up each team fit.

title: Divine intervention? How Dallas landed Dak Prescott at No. 135 in the 2016 NFL draft
content: As the moment neared, OC Scott Linehan was holding his breath, thinking, "Someone else is going to take him."

title: 'He's reinvigorated': A rare long offseason has given Andy Reid time to make changes
content: The NFL's oldest and longest-tenured coach faces a task he hasn't seen in a long time -- a bounce-back season.

title: From Cyndi Lauper to 'The Man': The evolution of women's wrestling at WrestleMania
content: Dive into how women's professional wrestling has evolved on WWE's biggest stage over the past 40 years.

title: Dickie V's Dazzling Dozen: Players, performances, coaches that defined the season
content: Dick Vitale hands out superlatives for a season that had everything, from Diaper Dandies to Prime-Time Performers.

title: Inside the Bucks' lost season, and the looming 'nasty breakup' with Giannis Antetokounmpo
content: A difficult season exacerbated a 13-year relationship that was already on the rocks, leading to more questions than answers in Milwaukee.

title: Connelly's favorite transfer classes: Teams that pushed the envelope in the portal
content: Looking beyond the numbers, here are the teams that took the boldest steps in their portal approaches.

title: Six things I wish I'd known when I drafted
content: Tristan H. Cockcroft takes a look at biggest fantasy surprises from the first few weeks of the season.

title: Prospect watch: Konnor Griffin's here, who's next?
content: All the prospects fantasy managers should be keeping an eye on as the season moves through its first month.

title: Baseball in Latin America is broken -- is an international draft the answer?
content: As the search for the next Dominican baseball phenom intensifies, the tragic death of a teenager is the latest symptom of a failing system.

title: Pochettino's to-do list: What the USMNT must figure out before World Cup
content: There are no more friendlies or camps before the U.S. names its World Cup roster, so what does coach Mauricio Pochettino need to do before then?

title: NWSL Power Rankings: San Diego leads the way, Washington back on track
content: San Diego Wave are the new leaders of the ESPN's NWSL Power Rankings, while the Washington Spirit are soaring up.

title: PGA Tour vs. LIV in a much different place than it was at 2023 Masters
content: It's been three years, and while the LIV-PGA fight isn't necessarily 'won,' it certainly seems settled.

title: The good, the bad and the ugly of F1's new 2026 regulations
content: F1's new-for-2026 regulations have come in for considerable criticism, but there have been positives, too. ESPN weighs up the good and the bad.

title: Women's boxing pound-for-pound rankings: Two fighters make their top-10 debut
content: Two fighters break into the top 10 after standout performances. Here are the latest women's pound-for-pound rankings.

title: Men's divisional rankings: Why Wilder's win wasn't enough to join top 10 at heavyweight
content: The Deontay Wilder-Derek Chisora fight card caused a major shakeup, but not in the division you would think.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Chelsea linked with move for El Mala
content: Chelsea are reportedly lining up a move to sign FC Cologne forward Said El Mala. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Assam election 2026 LIVE: 17.87% voter turnout recorded till 9 a.m.
content: The river island of Majuli recorded the highest turnout in the first two hours at 20.03%, while Tamulpur in the Bodoland Territorial Region witnessed the lowest at 15.32%

title: Kerala election 2026 LIVE updates: Polling progresses in 140 constituencies; Pinarayi says confident of 'bigger' LDF win; UDF eyes comeback
content: Over 2.7 crore voters are eligible to vote across the State, where the elections are being held in a single phase for all 140 constituencies, and 883 candidates are in the fray

title: Kerala Assembly elections 2026 | Constituency watch
content: A collection of stories on some of the key constituencies in Kerala that are set to elect their representatives to Kerala Legislative Assembly in 2026

title: BJP, the known unknown in Kerala 2026
content: If the voting gets polarised on the singular question of whether to give Pinarayi Vijayan a third consecutive term, the saffron party could be marginalised

title: Tanker carrying 15,400 tonnes of LPG arrives at JNPA in Navi Mumbai
content: This is a significant milestone as it is the first such vessel to reach JNPA since the onset of the war, JNPA said in a statement

title: Poll-bound Assam trails in health and education indicators
content: Low basic vaccination, high maternal mortality, and increasing dropout rates at the higher education level are some of the challenges the State faces

title: Why does India want fast breeder nuclear reactors? | Explained
content: Most of India’s current reactors are pressurised heavy water reactors, which are inefficient because only a small fraction of the fuel, around 1%, undergoes fission before it becomes unusable

title: Karnataka bypolls at 9 am: 9% polling in Davanagere South, 11.87% in Bagalkot
content: The polls were necessitated in Bagalkot and Davanagere South by the deaths of senior Congress MLAs H.Y. Meti and Shamanur Shivashankarappa respectively

title: Kerala goes to polls today with 2.71 crore voters, 883 candidates
content: The 2026 elections have also drawn attention as it is the first to be held after the SIR of the electoral roll

title: Bosch Limited to acquire Bosch Chassis Systems India for ₹9,068.68 crore
content: Bosch Chassis Systems is part of Bosch’s Vehicle Motion division in India and is one of the market leaders in automotive safety systems

title: Kerala Assembly elections: Pinarayi Vijayan says confident of ‘bigger’ LDF win
content: CM said Kerala is on a strong path towards becoming a developed State, with achievements across sectors that must continue uninterrupted

title: All set for polling for Davangere South, Bagalkot byelections on April 9
content: A total of 34 candidates are in fray for the byelections — nine in Bagalkot and 25 in Davangere South constituency

title: Star Health data leak case: Madras High Court dismisses appeals by Punjab-based hacker
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan refuse to entertain six writ appeals filed by Himanshu Pathak

title: Consumer court tells insurer to pay claim of Udupi boat stricken by Cyclone Phyan in Karwar that drifted to Maharashtra
content: After a legal battle of more than 16 years, Karnataka State Consumer Disputed Redressal Commission directed the insurance company to pay 75% of the claim, fine and litigation costs

title: Nagaland: Voting for Koridang Assembly bypoll begins amid tight security
content: Six candidates are in the fray for the Assembly bypoll necessitated by the death of sitting BJP MLA Imkong L. Imchen

title: When war feels next door
content: An oil rig worker from West Bengal recounts his life in Kuwait as U.S.-Israel-Iran war draws in, with fear, uncertainty and no clear way home

title: Gujarat hosts CWG review team; preparations for 2030 games under focus
content: The visit began with a meeting between the delegation and Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel in Gandhinagar, where preparations, execution strategy and timelines were discussed

title: GIFT City fund ecosystem expands sharply; capital commitments cross $32 billion
content: The increase marks nearly a 60-fold jump, reflecting the growing use of the international financial services centre for structuring India-focused and global investment strategies

title: These ‘Soil Sakhis’ in Maharashtra’s drought prone region are bringing about a quiet revolution
content: How empowered marginalised women are helping farmers increase their yield in this drought-prone region in Maharashtra. Scientists and farmers’ bodies validate their contribution for changing farmers’ lives

title: TMC government snatched Bengal people's right to land, gave those to Bangladeshi infiltrators: Nabin
content: Addressing an election rally in north Bengal's Alipurduar, he said once the BJP forms a "double-engine" government in the State, it will rectify the demographic alterations and throw out Bangladeshi infiltrators from the State

title: NCP-SP leader Samarjeetsinh Ghatge rejoins BJP
content: Samarjeetsinh Ghatge’s credentials are likely to help the BJP strengthen its wings in the sugar-belt districts of Pune, Kolhapur, Sangli, and Satara

title: Logic may not be the right tool to examine faith and belief systems, says Supreme Court
content: Nine-judge Bench headed by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant continues hearing on Sabarimala entry issue

title: Kharge expresses regret over ‘illiterate’ remark, says he respects people of Gujarat
content: Congress leader Mallikarjun Kharge apologises for calling Gujaratis ‘illiterate’, reaffirming his respect for the State’s people

title: Odisha Law Commission recommends a new law on hate speech, hate crime
content: It is recommended that person repeating crime related to hate speech and hate crime may have to undergo two to seven years of jail term with pecuniary fine of ₹50,000

title: IGoM reviews preparedness amid West Asia developments, ensures energy, food security
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh says government is ensuring uninterrupted availability of LPG, petrol, diesel and fertilizers, while maintaining supplies of essential commodities

title: States to be pushed for ‘utility led aggregation’ to meet PM Surya Ghar targets
content: Under this model, State electricity distribution companies will pay for installing rooftop solar systems in households that either can’t afford them or don’t have adequate infrastructure to instal rooftop systems

title: India-Turkiye talks hint at a thaw in bilateral relations
content: Talks follow consultations with Azerbaijan; indicate shift in New Delhi’s position on ties with countries supporting Pakistan

title: Humayun Kabir’s helicopter ride makes waves as queries emerge over its funding
content: “I have to address about 50 political rallies in the State and about 20 of them are with Owaisi saheb,” says the Aam Janata Unnyan Party founder and suspended Trinamool Congress leader

title: Sasmit Patra offers to quit as BJD Parliamentary Party leader in Rajya Sabha
content: While the immediate reason for Mr. Patra’s decision remains unclear, several BJD Rajya Sabha MPs are learnt to have favoured his removal from the leadership role

title: West Bengal Assembly elections: Promise of jobs remains big highlight of poll manifestos of Left Front and Congress
content: Left Front manifesto promises initiatives for heavy and medium scale industrialisation, setting up of new software and hardware parks; Congress manifesto looks like a copy of the Trinamool Congress poll promises

title: Stage set for a keen contest, as Mamata files papers from Bhabanipur
content: The BJP’s candidate from Bhabanipur is the Leader of the Opposition in the West Bengal Assembly Suvendu Adhikari, who claims, ‘She and her party both will lose this time’

title: Edappadi K. Palaniswami, the lone star of AIADMK election campaign, steps up his pitch for power
content: The former Chief Minister is the main campaigner for the AIADMK across Tamil Nadu, with a stump speech focused on law and order issues, the State’s finances, concerns of government employees, drug menace, and rising inflation; he also highlights features of the manifesto of his party

title: Steps into past: 1,500-year-old reservoir unearthed on Elephanta island
content: Archaeological Survey of India excavation finds sophisticated stepped reservoir and proof of ancient maritime trade

title: Textbook row: academics ask President to intervene
content: By forcefully intervening in what is essentially an educational matter, threatening criminal contempt of court and banishing respected educationists without hearing them, the Supreme Court, had encroached upon academic freedom, the letter says

title: India welcomes Pakistan-brokered Iran-U.S. ceasefire, stresses dialogue and diplomacy
content: Government calls for de-escalation and unimpeded navigation through the Strait of Hormuz, does not acknowledge Pakistan’s role in U.S.-Iran ceasefire talks

title: Election Commission-Trinamool Congress meeting ends on an acrimonious note
content: Trinamool delegation says Chief Election Commissioner told them to ‘get lost’, poll body sources say the Commission asked Rajya Sabha Member Derek O’Brien not to shout and ‘maintain decorum’

title: Despite having nine large waterbodies, residents do not get clean groundwater
content: They say that if the government removes encroachments and brings excess water from Chembarambakkam to the lakes in the constituency, the drinking water problem can be solved; another issue is that stray animal population keeps rising though the authorities take steps to catch them

title: Every town to get basic sports infra in next three years, says A.P. Minister
content: Development work at Indira Gandhi Municipal Corporation Stadium is being taken at an initial estimated cost of around ₹170 crore, says Ramprasad Reddy.

title: Amaravati will be developed as global creative economy hub, says Minister
content: Kandula Durgesh discusses integrating content, culture, lifestyle and immersive design into tourism development with stakeholders at Hotel Investment Conference South Asia in Mumbai

title: Priyank Kharge directs zilla panchayat CEOs to ensure there is no drinking water shortage in Karnataka villages
content: As Karnataka is facing the peak summer heat, Minister for Rural Development and Panchayat Raj and IT and BT Priyank Kharge on Wednesday directed Chief Executive Officers of all zilla panchayats to take urgent and proactive measures to ensure that no village faces a shortage of drinking water during the summer months

title: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah accuses Election Commission of India of being ‘selective and complicit’
content: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah has accused the Election Commission of India of being ‘selective and complicit’ in its approach

title: Pune Doctor, 75, Loses Over Rs 12 Crore In Online Trading Scam
content: A Pune doctor lost Rs 12.31 crore in an online share trading scam involving a fake app and WhatsApp group.

title: Insider Leak? Betting Accounts Earn Big On Trump's Ceasefire Post
content: Such bets have repeatedly raised questions from the public as well as members of Congress about whether some traders are using inside information

title: Indian-Origin "Ketamine Queen" Jailed For Selling Matthew Perry Drugs
content: Jasveen Sangha, 42, a dual citizen of the United States and Britain, was jailed on Wednesday for her role in the actor's 2023 overdose death.

title: "Who's This Segal?": Supreme Court On Centre's Objection To DY Chandrachud's View On Adultery
content: Citing foreign articles and judgments quoted by former Chief Justice DY Chandrachud in his verdict decriminalising adultery, the Centre said that "blanket reliance on international judgments is impermissible"

title: Voting Underway For Karnataka Bypolls In Bagalkot And Davanagere South
content: Karnataka Bypolls 2026: Voting is underway for bypolls in Bagalkot and Davanagere South in Karnataka on Thursday.

title: Struggling With Loans? A Step-By-Step Guide On How To Secure 750+ CIBIL Score
content: A high score signals lower risk to lenders, while a low score suggests a borrower may struggle to meet repayment obligations.

title: Nagaland Bypoll: 6 Candidates To Fight For Koridang Assembly Seat
content: Nagaland Bypoll 2026: Koridang assembly bypoll begins with heightened security after recent election violence.

title: Tripura Bypoll: 6 Candidates To Fight For Dharmanagar Assembly Seat
content: Tripura Bypoll 2026: Voting began at 7 am for Dharmanagar Assembly byelection amid tight security and webcasting.

title: In 1st Polls After Delimitation, BJP Hopes For Third Straight Term In Assam: 10 Points
content: The battle for 126 seats in Assam comes amid a bitter political row between the ruling BJP and the Opposition Congress over the corruption allegations against Chief Minister Himanta Sarma. The BJP is expecting a third straight term in the state.

title: 'Ketamine Queen' Jailed For 15 Years For Selling Drugs To Matthew Perry
content: Jasveen Sangha, 42, was one of five people charged over the death of the beloved Canadian-American actor.

title: TVK To Play Spoiler For Congress-DMK As Puducherry Votes Today? 10 Points
content: The NDA is hoping for a second straight term in Puducherry, where 30 seats are up for election today. The pitch, though, has been queered by the entry of the fledgling TVK, which has set up multi-cornered contests in many seats.

title: Congress Waits In The Wings As CPM Crosses Fingers For A Third Term In Kerala
content: The election for 140 assembly seats in Kerala today is set to be a riveting contest for a state that threw a surprise in the last election. There is speculation that today's single-phase election could be more unpredictable than the one in 2021.

title: "Political Disaster": Israel Opposition Blasts Netanyahu Over Iran Ceasefire

title: India Enters New Nuclear Era As Kalpakkam Reactor Attains Criticality
content: With this achievement, the country formally entered the second stage of the three-stage nuclear power programme envisioned by Homi J Bhabha.

title: Iran Announces Alternative Routes In Hormuz Strait
content: Iran announced alternative routes on Thursday for ships travelling through the Strait of Hormuz, citing the risk of sea mines in the main zone of the vital waterway.

title: North Korea Says Latest Tests Used Cluster-Bomb Warhead Missiles
content: The report by North Korean state media came a day after South Korea's military said it detected North Korea firing multiple missiles from an eastern coastal area in its second round of launches in two days.

title: Rankings: Profiling more than 60 wide receivers
content: Mike Clay is revealing his fantasy football rookie rankings at each key offensive position . the rankings do not include rookies because we are unsure of their landing spots . all references to player ages are as of the season opener on sept. 9, 2026 .

title: PSG's wastefulness leaves door open, but this isn't Klopp's Liverpool any more
content: Ousmane Dembélé has a history of giving Liverpool hope when all seems lost in a UEFA Champions League knockout tie . in 2019, with Barcelona 3-0 up in the dying seconds of their semifinal first leg against Liverpool at Camp Nou, dembélé blazed an easy chance to make it 4-0 over the crossbar . we all know what happened next .

title: 'I'm going to put it in my house in a safe': How the Masters lawn gnome became so popular that it might be discontinued
content: Ryan Carey is considered the world's most accomplished golf memorabilia expert . the gnome has been a kitschy decorative item for centuries . it's the only item available on site that is limited to one purchase per patron .

title: Frozen Four: How Denver, Michigan, Wisconsin and North Dakota can win it all
content: the 2026 men's Frozen Four is set, with Michigan and Denver joining North Dakota and Wisconsin in the field bound for Las Vegas to play for the national championship . the Pioneers avenged last season's loss to Western Michigan with a 6-2 win over the defending national champs to win the Loveland Regional .

title: WNBA mock draft: UConn's Fudd drops to No. 3 pick
content: Olivia Miles drops 28 points, 10 rebounds and eight assists as TCU puts away Virginia 79-69 . the college players available in Monday's draft had one last audition in the tournament . in our latest WNBA mock draft, for example, Miles projected as a lottery pick last season when she was eligible age-wise .

title: 'Don't move on, just move forward': How hockey helped Michigan's Michael Hage overcome tragedy
content: the path to a national title runs through the site of the Frozen Four . it was in Las Vegas where one of the most significant moments of Hage's hockey career occurred . the 19-year-old center doesn't remember everything about his draft night at the Sphere in June 2024 .

title: NBA Futures: Lakers' championship odds now 300-1
content: los Angeles Lakers were 30-1 for the title, but a major injury to Luka Doncic lengthened them to 100-1 . the lakers are now 300-1 to win the championship this season . los Angeles continues to approach odds-on title status, moving from +135 to +120 .

title: Women's transfer rankings for 2026-27 season
content: the women's college basketball transfer portal opened Monday with a tidal wave of activity . more than 1,300 players are looking for new homes . the portal closes April 20 but players' decisions for their next school can still come after that .

title: Way-Too-Early Top 25: Ranking the best teams for next season, now
content: the 2026-27 college basketball season is expected to take place in 2026 . we're already rolling right into the "offseason," which mostly involves rampant roster changes for the next six weeks . in the past, we've had pretty hard-and-fast house rules regarding the Way-Too-Early Top 25 .

title: NBA Power Rankings: Where all 30 teams land
content: the end of the 2025-26 regular season is finally here . the campaign officially coming to a close this Sunday with all 30 teams playing . every new season comes with high expectations, with some teams meeting or exceeding those goals and others falling far from the original game plan .
